input.prompt:
I'm coming from ASP.NET Core and I registered my DbContext with
AddScoped in my MAUI app. My data seems to persist across page
navigations when it shouldn't. What's going on?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm coming from ASP.NET Core and I registered my DbContext with
AddScoped in my MAUI app. My data seems to persist across page
navigations when it shouldn't. What's going on?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common gotcha when transitioning from ASP.NET Core to .NET MAUI regarding dependency injection scoping — specifically why `AddScoped` behaves differently in MAUI than in ASP.NET Core.

This is a conceptual question I can answer directly without needing to search any codebase.

In ASP.NET Core, a "scope" is tied to an HTTP request — each request gets its own scope, so `AddScoped` services are created per-request and disposed at the end of the request.

In .NET MAUI, there's no concept of an HTTP request. The DI container has a single scope for the entire application lifetime. 

This means `AddScoped` effectively acts like `AddSingleton` in MAUI — your DbContext gets created once and reused throughout the app's lifetime, which is why data appears to persist across page navigations. To fix this, I'd either switch to `AddTransient` to create a fresh DbContext each time it's needed, or manually manage scopes using `IServiceScopeFactory`.
